name: Aloha Kitchen
tagline: Famous for impossibly fluffy soufflé pancakes — Japanese-Hawaiian brunch fusion near Ala Moana.
mustOrder: soufflé pancakes — the signature, jiggly and cloud-like
parking: Street parking on Ena Rd; Ala Moana Center parking nearby
bestTime: Weekday breakfast. Weekend brunch has a 30–60 min wait. The soufflé pancakes take 20 min to make regardless
talkStory: Aloha Kitchen is where Japanese soufflé pancake culture meets Hawaiian comfort food. The pancakes are the draw — impossibly tall, jiggly, cloud-like creations that take 20 minutes to prepare (they warn you upfront). But the rest of the menu is solid local comfort food: loco moco, spam plates, and curry. Popular with Japanese tourists who know the soufflé pancake genre, but locals come for the loco moco. Be prepared to wait on weekends.
